message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_digamma autograd via apply_function with trigamma backward [bd-1tax]

tensor_digamma had a fail-loud guard rejecting tracked inputs. PyTorch's
torch.special.digamma is differentiable with d/dx digamma(x) = trigamma(x)
= polygamma(1, x).

Wrap digamma_approx in tensor_apply_function with a polygamma_approx(1, x)
backward closure — same pattern as the gammaln autograd wiring (7cc0b45).

    forward : y = digamma(x)
    backward: dy/dx = polygamma(1, x) = trigamma(x)

Test: digamma_propagates_gradient_via_trigamma asserts on input
[1, 2, 3] under sum loss that grad equals trigamma at each x within
1e-4 absolute (polygamma_approx is looser than digamma_approx — its
asymptotic envelope is empirically ~5e-6 at small integer x; the
bound still catches sign-flip and missing-factorial-term regressions
without pretending to a precision the implementation doesn't deliver).

All existing digamma tests (digamma_known_values,
digamma_signed_zero_returns_signed_infinity,
digamma_negative_integer_poles_return_nan, plus the upstream
gammaln_propagates_gradient_via_digamma which exercises digamma's
forward through gammaln's backward) continue to pass — bit-for-bit
forward parity preserved.

Files closure for the digamma slice of frankentorch-1tax.

Workspace: 1130 ft-api tests green (1129 + 1 new), clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
